_meta.smokeTest: lesson_02 example sentences 5 件 (Phase 5 ⑤ skill 実機初投入)

vocabulary.prompt:
[PURPOSE]
Create a vocabulary card illustration for Japanese language learning materials.
The image must instantly communicate the meaning of the word "医者" at a glance.

[SUBJECT]
A specific adult person. light brown skin with short curly dark-brown hair. Outfit and props: white doctor's coat (open at the front) over a simple blouse or scrubs top; MUST visibly wear a stethoscope draped around the neck with the chestpiece resting on the chest; MUST visibly wear a small name badge clipped to the coat (no specific name written, just a generic badge silhouette); MAY also hold a clipboard or pen in one hand as a secondary prop. Footwear MUST be plain dark leather loafers. Pose and expression: calm professional standing pose with arms naturally at the sides, neutral and welcoming expression.
The character's role must be immediately obvious from clothing, posture, and props alone.
FACIAL FEATURES RULE (v4.0 PART 1.8): The character's face MUST clearly show all four primary facial features — eyes (with visible pupils, not blank circles), eyebrows (simple curved or angled lines above the eyes), nose (a simple line or dot or small shape), and mouth (a simple line or curve or small shape). NEVER omit any of these four features.
HEAD-BODY PROPORTION RULE (v4.0 PART 1.10): The character MUST be rendered as an adult standing figure with approximately 7-head-height proportion. NEVER render adult roles with shorter 5-6 head proportions.

[SCENE & ACTION]
ASPECT RATIO: The output image MUST be 1:1 SQUARE.
FULL-BODY SHOT — the entire figure from the very top of the head to the soles of BOTH feet is fully inside the frame. head-to-toe figure spans about 80% of the image HEIGHT.
Camera angle: HORIZONTAL 3/4 view at EYE LEVEL.
FOOTWEAR RULE: Both feet MUST visibly wear footwear.
Solid soft cream off-white background (warm off-white, NOT pure stark white). No other characters or objects in the frame.
Framed with a standard ~50mm-equivalent lens at full standing distance.

[STYLE RECIPE — DO NOT CHANGE]
Minimalist flat vector illustration. Clean continuous black outlines with consistent line weight. Completely flat solid color fills only. Color palette: soft cream off-white background (warm off-white, NOT pure stark white), deep slate navy outlines, muted warm blue and warm amber gold as accents, cool slate gray for secondary elements.
This should look like it belongs in a brand style guide, not like AI art. Keep line weights consistent.

[CONSTRAINTS]
No text, no letters, no numbers, no decorative symbols anywhere.
The clothing, accessories, props, and any visible badges must NEVER include any flag, national emblem, nationality pin, country indicator, political symbol, or red-and-white circular badge. This is a role-based vocabulary card, not a nationality card.
No gradients, no shadows, no 3D effects, no photoreal textures.

[PURPOSE]
Create an example sentence illustration for Japanese language learning materials.
The image must clearly convey the grammatical relationship in the sentence:
[これは時計です。] (This is a clock.)

[SUBJECT]
One young East Asian adult female in her 20s with long straight dark-brown to black hair (mid-length to longer, falling around the shoulders). She wears casual smart everyday clothing — a simple plain muted warm blue or cool slate gray short-sleeve or long-sleeve top paired with plain dark slim jeans or slacks. Footwear MUST be plain low-profile white canvas sneakers. Her expression is bright and open — friendly language-learner vibe. East Asian features typical of a young woman from Northeast Asia.
FACIAL FEATURES RULE (v4.0 PART 1.8): Every character figure in the image MUST clearly show all four primary facial features — eyes (with visible pupils, not blank circles), eyebrows (simple curved or angled lines above the eyes), nose (a simple line or dot or small shape), and mouth (a simple line or curve or small shape). NEVER omit any of these four features. A faceless silhouette, blank face, partially-rendered face, or 'stylized stock illustration without facial detail' is NOT permitted, even when the outfit (business suit, doctor coat, uniform, etc.) is the dominant role signature. Facial features must be drawn in the same flat illustration style as the rest of the body — minimal but visibly present.
HEAD-BODY PROPORTION RULE (v4.0 PART 1.10): Every adult character figure MUST be rendered with approximately 7-head-height proportion (head height = approximately 1/7 of total body height). NEVER render adult roles with shorter 5-6 head proportions which read as childlike or cartoonish. This is especially important for figures with childhood-bias triggers — business suit + briefcase, school-style casual + backpack, uniforms — where nanobanana tends to default to anime salaryman/student cliché short proportions. Adult characters (approximately 20s-50s) must read as working-age adults by body proportion alone. Exception: explicit child NAMED_CHARACTERs with documented child age_range follow their own fixed_features.
FOOTWEAR RULE (v3.11 / preserved in v4.0): Every character figure MUST visibly wear footwear on both feet (shoes, sandals, sneakers, loafers, work boots, or similar) whenever both feet are inside the frame. Barefoot is NOT permitted in example-sentence illustrations — even when the cultural styling implies an at-home or indoor context. If the composition crops feet out of frame (waist-up shots etc.), this rule does not apply to the cropped figures.

[SCENE & ACTION]
She stands behind a simple plain wooden desk in the foreground that occupies the lower third of the frame. Her right arm extends forward at chest height with the index finger pointing directly down at — and almost touching — a round analog wall-style clock that rests flat on the desk surface directly in front of her, well within her arm's length. The clock is the secondary focal item: a clear circular face about 18-22% of the frame width, deep slate navy outline rim, plain pale-cream face with two simple thin black hour and minute hands and twelve plain dot tick marks (absolutely no numerals, no brand name, no text of any kind on the clock face). Her body language and the clock's position firmly within her personal space communicate the demonstrative これ (kore, near-speaker) — the object is on her side of the desk and inside her arm's-length reach.
The characters' actions and relationship must make the sentence meaning visually obvious without any text.

Composition: Wide 16:9 shot, 50mm standard lens equivalent (natural perspective).
Characters occupy 60% of the frame.
The young woman is positioned slightly right of center, her full standing body visible from head to soles of both feet inside the frame. The clock on the desk is in the lower-left third, the pointing hand bridges the two. Empty muted background on either side gives the viewer a clean read of the gesture.
Eye-level view. Simple minimal background with only essential context.
Main characters are clearly separated from the background in visual contrast.

[STYLE RECIPE — DO NOT CHANGE]
Minimalist flat vector illustration. Clean continuous black outlines with consistent line weight.
Completely flat solid color fills only. Color palette: soft cream off-white background (warm off-white, NOT pure stark white),
deep slate navy outlines, muted warm colors,
warm amber gold accents for emphasis.
This should look like it belongs in a brand style guide, not like AI art. Keep line weights consistent.

[CONSTRAINTS]
No text, no letters, no numbers, no purely decorative symbols inside the image. VISUAL_SYMBOLS entries (question mark / checkmark / X mark / arrow) ARE PERMITTED when explicitly described in [SCENE & ACTION].
No gradients, no shadows, no 3D effects, no photoreal textures.
Apply zero ambient lighting, zero drop shadows, zero global illumination.

vocabulary.reading: あれはエービーシービルです。
vocabulary.en: That over there is the ABC Building.
vocabulary.prompt:
[PURPOSE]
Create an example sentence illustration for Japanese language learning materials.
The image must clearly convey the grammatical relationship in the sentence:
[あれはABCビルです。] (That over there is the ABC Building.)

[SUBJECT]
One young East Asian adult female in her 20s with long straight dark-brown to black hair (mid-length to longer, falling around the shoulders). She wears casual smart everyday clothing — a simple plain muted warm blue or cool slate gray short-sleeve or long-sleeve top paired with plain dark slim jeans or slacks. Footwear MUST be plain low-profile white canvas sneakers. Her expression is bright and open — friendly language-learner vibe. East Asian features typical of a young woman from Northeast Asia.
FACIAL FEATURES RULE (v4.0 PART 1.8): Every character figure in the image MUST clearly show all four primary facial features — eyes (with visible pupils, not blank circles), eyebrows (simple curved or angled lines above the eyes), nose (a simple line or dot or small shape), and mouth (a simple line or curve or small shape). NEVER omit any of these four features. A faceless silhouette, blank face, partially-rendered face, or 'stylized stock illustration without facial detail' is NOT permitted, even when the outfit (business suit, doctor coat, uniform, etc.) is the dominant role signature. Facial features must be drawn in the same flat illustration style as the rest of the body — minimal but visibly present.
HEAD-BODY PROPORTION RULE (v4.0 PART 1.10): Every adult character figure MUST be rendered with approximately 7-head-height proportion (head height = approximately 1/7 of total body height). NEVER render adult roles with shorter 5-6 head proportions which read as childlike or cartoonish. This is especially important for figures with childhood-bias triggers — business suit + briefcase, school-style casual + backpack, uniforms — where nanobanana tends to default to anime salaryman/student cliché short proportions. Adult characters (approximately 20s-50s) must read as working-age adults by body proportion alone. Exception: explicit child NAMED_CHARACTERs with documented child age_range follow their own fixed_features.
FOOTWEAR RULE (v3.11 / preserved in v4.0): Every character figure MUST visibly wear footwear on both feet (shoes, sandals, sneakers, loafers, work boots, or similar) whenever both feet are inside the frame. Barefoot is NOT permitted in example-sentence illustrations — even when the cultural styling implies an at-home or indoor context. If the composition crops feet out of frame (waist-up shots etc.), this rule does not apply to the cropped figures.

[SCENE & ACTION]
She stands outdoors on a city sidewalk in the foreground, her right arm extended forward and slightly upward with the index finger pointing into the distance toward a tall modern office building visible far away in the background. The building is rendered small (about 15-20% of the frame width by its visible portion) to clearly communicate distance — a clean rectangular silhouette with a regular grid of windows, a flat top, and a single short ENGLISH signage label reading "ABC" placed centered on the building's facade near the top (one short English word only, deep slate navy lettering on a plain panel, no other text, no Japanese characters anywhere). The visible distance between her and the building — the building is several blocks away — firmly communicates the demonstrative あれ (are, far from both speaker and listener).
The characters' actions and relationship must make the sentence meaning visually obvious without any text.

Composition: Wide 16:9 shot, 50mm standard lens equivalent (natural perspective).
Characters occupy 60% of the frame.
The young woman occupies the left third of the frame standing in 3/4 view facing right, her full body visible head to soles of both feet inside the frame. The ABC Building stands in the upper right third, much smaller in apparent size due to perspective. A long thin cool slate gray pointing-arrow guide-line (per ARROW_SEMIOTICS straight_long_thin) MAY be drawn very subtly along the line of her gaze toward the building to reinforce the 'pointing into the distance' reading.
Eye-level view. Simple minimal background with only essential context.
Main characters are clearly separated from the background in visual contrast.

[STYLE RECIPE — DO NOT CHANGE]
Minimalist flat vector illustration. Clean continuous black outlines with consistent line weight.
Completely flat solid color fills only. Color palette: soft cream off-white background (warm off-white, NOT pure stark white),
deep slate navy outlines, muted warm colors,
warm amber gold accents for emphasis.
This should look like it belongs in a brand style guide, not like AI art. Keep line weights consistent.

[CONSTRAINTS]
No text, no letters, no numbers, no purely decorative symbols inside the image. VISUAL_SYMBOLS entries (question mark / checkmark / X mark / arrow) ARE PERMITTED when explicitly described in [SCENE & ACTION].
No gradients, no shadows, no 3D effects, no photoreal textures.
Apply zero ambient lighting, zero drop shadows, zero global illumination.

vocabulary.en: That over there is a mountain.
vocabulary.prompt:
[PURPOSE]
Create an example sentence illustration for Japanese language learning materials.
The image must clearly convey the grammatical relationship in the sentence:
[あれは山です。] (That over there is a mountain.)

[SUBJECT]
One young East Asian adult female in her 20s with long straight dark-brown to black hair (mid-length to longer, falling around the shoulders). She wears casual smart everyday clothing — a simple plain muted warm blue or cool slate gray short-sleeve or long-sleeve top paired with plain dark slim jeans or slacks. Facial features must be drawn in the same flat illustration style as the rest of the body — minimal but visibly present.
HEAD-BODY PROPORTION RULE (v4.0 PART 1.10): Every adult character figure MUST be rendered with approximately 7-head-height proportion (head height = approximately 1/7 of total body height). NEVER render adult roles with shorter 5-6 head proportions which read as childlike or cartoonish. This is especially important for figures with childhood-bias triggers — business suit + briefcase, school-style casual + backpack, uniforms — where nanobanana tends to default to anime salaryman/student cliché short proportions. Adult characters (approximately 20s-50s) must read as working-age adults by body proportion alone. Exception: explicit child NAMED_CHARACTERs with documented child age_range follow their own fixed_features.
FOOTWEAR RULE (v3.11 / preserved in v4.0): Every character figure MUST visibly wear footwear on both feet (shoes, sandals, sneakers, loafers, work boots, or similar) whenever both feet are inside the frame. Barefoot is NOT permitted in example-sentence illustrations — even when the cultural styling implies an at-home or indoor context. If the composition crops feet out of frame (waist-up shots etc.), this rule does not apply to the cropped figures.

[SCENE & ACTION]
She stands outdoors in an open foreground area — a simple grassy or paved flat ground — with her right arm extended forward and slightly upward, index finger pointing into the distance toward a mountain visible far away in the background. The mountain is rendered as a single clear simple symmetrical triangular silhouette in muted cool slate gray (about 25-30% of the frame width by its base, peak in the upper-middle of the frame), with no detailed texture, no snowcap-specific markings (a small lighter triangle at the peak is acceptable), and no other landscape clutter. The vast empty space between her and the mountain firmly communicates the demonstrative あれ (are, far from both speaker and listener).
The characters' actions and relationship must make the sentence meaning visually obvious without any text.

Composition: Wide 16:9 shot, 50mm standard lens equivalent (natural perspective).
Characters occupy 60% of the frame.
The young woman occupies the lower-left third of the frame, full body visible from head to soles of both feet. The mountain silhouette sits centered-to-right in the background mid-to-upper portion. The horizon line passes through her waist level. A simple subtle horizon line separates the foreground ground from the background sky-toned negative space.
Eye-level view. Simple minimal background with only essential context.
Main characters are clearly separated from the background in visual contrast.

[STYLE RECIPE — DO NOT CHANGE]
Minimalist flat vector illustration. Clean continuous black outlines with consistent line weight.
Completely flat solid color fills only. Color palette: soft cream off-white background (warm off-white, NOT pure stark white),
deep slate navy outlines, muted warm colors,
warm amber gold accents for emphasis.
This should look like it belongs in a brand style guide, not like AI art. Keep line weights consistent.

[CONSTRAINTS]
No text, no letters, no numbers, no purely decorative symbols inside the image. VISUAL_SYMBOLS entries (question mark / checkmark / X mark / arrow) ARE PERMITTED when explicitly described in [SCENE & ACTION].
No gradients, no shadows, no 3D effects, no photoreal textures.
Apply zero ambient lighting, zero drop shadows, zero global illumination.

[PURPOSE]
Create an example sentence illustration for Japanese language learning materials.
The image must clearly convey the grammatical relationship in the sentence:
[何ですか。] (What is it?)

[SUBJECT]
One young East Asian adult female in her 20s with long straight dark-brown to black hair (mid-length to longer, falling around the shoulders). She wears casual smart everyday clothing — a simple plain muted warm blue or cool slate gray short-sleeve or long-sleeve top paired with plain dark slim jeans or slacks. Footwear MUST be plain low-profile white canvas sneakers. Her expression is bright and open — friendly language-learner vibe. East Asian features typical of a young woman from Northeast Asia.
FACIAL FEATURES RULE (v4.0 PART 1.8): Every character figure in the image MUST clearly show all four primary facial features — eyes (with visible pupils, not blank circles), eyebrows (simple curved or angled lines above the eyes), nose (a simple line or dot or small shape), and mouth (a simple line or curve or small shape). NEVER omit any of these four features. A faceless silhouette, blank face, partially-rendered face, or 'stylized stock illustration without facial detail' is NOT permitted, even when the outfit (business suit, doctor coat, uniform, etc.) is the dominant role signature. Facial features must be drawn in the same flat illustration style as the rest of the body — minimal but visibly present.
HEAD-BODY PROPORTION RULE (v4.0 PART 1.10): Every adult character figure MUST be rendered with approximately 7-head-height proportion (head height = approximately 1/7 of total body height). NEVER render adult roles with shorter 5-6 head proportions which read as childlike or cartoonish. This is especially important for figures with childhood-bias triggers — business suit + briefcase, school-style casual + backpack, uniforms — where nanobanana tends to default to anime salaryman/student cliché short proportions. Adult characters (approximately 20s-50s) must read as working-age adults by body proportion alone. Exception: explicit child NAMED_CHARACTERs with documented child age_range follow their own fixed_features.
FOOTWEAR RULE (v3.11 / preserved in v4.0): Every character figure MUST visibly wear footwear on both feet (shoes, sandals, sneakers, loafers, work boots, or similar) whenever both feet are inside the frame. Barefoot is NOT permitted in example-sentence illustrations — even when the cultural styling implies an at-home or indoor context. If the composition crops feet out of frame (waist-up shots etc.), this rule does not apply to the cropped figures.

[SCENE & ACTION]
She stands in the foreground looking down at a small unidentified flat-topped object placed on a simple plain surface (a low table or stool) in front of her. The unidentified object is rendered as a featureless soft-edged box shape in plain cool slate gray — deliberately ambiguous so the viewer reads it as 'unknown content'. Her right hand is raised palm-up at chest height in a small open questioning gesture, and her head is tilted slightly down toward the object. Directly above the unidentified object, floating in the upper-middle area of the frame, a single clear bold question mark symbol "?" is drawn in muted symbolic red (per PART 2 educational_symbol_colors.symbol_red) — clean flat shape, no shadow, no outline halo. The question mark is the VISUAL_SYMBOL cue that this sentence is interrogative.
The characters' actions and relationship must make the sentence meaning visually obvious without any text.

Composition: Wide 16:9 shot, 50mm standard lens equivalent (natural perspective).
Characters occupy 60% of the frame.
The young woman occupies the left half of the frame in 3/4 view facing slightly right, full body visible from head to soles of both feet. The small unknown object and the floating question mark are on the right half, the question mark in the upper-right quadrant directly above the object. The two figures (person and floating symbol) form a clear visual triangle pointing to the unknown object.
Eye-level view. Simple minimal background with only essential context.
Main characters are clearly separated from the background in visual contrast.

[STYLE RECIPE — DO NOT CHANGE]
Minimalist flat vector illustration. Clean continuous black outlines with consistent line weight.
Completely flat solid color fills only. Color palette: soft cream off-white background (warm off-white, NOT pure stark white),
deep slate navy outlines, muted warm colors,
warm amber gold accents for emphasis.
This should look like it belongs in a brand style guide, not like AI art. Keep line weights consistent.

[CONSTRAINTS]
No text, no letters, no numbers, no purely decorative symbols inside the image. VISUAL_SYMBOLS entries (question mark / checkmark / X mark / arrow) ARE PERMITTED when explicitly described in [SCENE & ACTION].
No gradients, no shadows, no 3D effects, no photoreal textures.

vocabulary.word: これはわたしのかばんです。
vocabulary.reading: これはわたしのかばんです。
vocabulary.prompt:
[PURPOSE]
Create an example sentence illustration for Japanese language learning materials.
The image must clearly convey the grammatical relationship in the sentence:
[これはわたしのかばんです。] (This is my bag.)

[SUBJECT]
One young East Asian adult female in her 20s with long straight dark-brown to black hair (mid-length to longer, falling around the shoulders). She wears casual smart everyday clothing — a simple plain muted warm blue or cool slate gray short-sleeve or long-sleeve top paired with plain dark slim jeans or slacks. Footwear MUST be plain low-profile white canvas sneakers. Her expression is bright and open — friendly language-learner vibe. East Asian features typical of a young woman from Northeast Asia.
FACIAL FEATURES RULE (v4.0 PART 1.8): Every character figure in the image MUST clearly show all four primary facial features — eyes (with visible pupils, not blank circles), eyebrows (simple curved or angled lines above the eyes), nose (a simple line or dot or small shape), and mouth (a simple line or curve or small shape). NEVER omit any of these four features. A faceless silhouette, blank face, partially-rendered face, or 'stylized stock illustration without facial detail' is NOT permitted, even when the outfit (business suit, doctor coat, uniform, etc.) is the dominant role signature. Facial features must be drawn in the same flat illustration style as the rest of the body — minimal but visibly present.
HEAD-BODY PROPORTION RULE (v4.0 PART 1.10): Every adult character figure MUST be rendered with approximately 7-head-height proportion (head height = approximately 1/7 of total body height). NEVER render adult roles with shorter 5-6 head proportions which read as childlike or cartoonish. This is especially important for figures with childhood-bias triggers — business suit + briefcase, school-style casual + backpack, uniforms — where nanobanana tends to default to anime salaryman/student cliché short proportions. Adult characters (approximately 20s-50s) must read as working-age adults by body proportion alone. Exception: explicit child NAMED_CHARACTERs with documented child age_range follow their own fixed_features.
FOOTWEAR RULE (v3.11 / preserved in v4.0): Every character figure MUST visibly wear footwear on both feet (shoes, sandals, sneakers, loafers, work boots, or similar) whenever both feet are inside the frame. Barefoot is NOT permitted in example-sentence illustrations — even when the cultural styling implies an at-home or indoor context. If the composition crops feet out of frame (waist-up shots etc.), this rule does not apply to the cropped figures.

[SCENE & ACTION]
She stands in the foreground holding a soft-bodied shoulder bag with both hands close to her torso — her left hand grips the bag's shoulder strap near the top, her right hand rests palm-down on the side of the bag at waist height in a clear "this is mine" possessive gesture. The bag is rendered as a simple rounded-rectangular soft-tote shape in warm amber gold (about 18-22% of the frame width, hanging at her hip level), with deep slate navy outline, a single strap visible over her right shoulder, plain front face with no logo, no badge, no text, no decoration. Her body language — the bag pulled in close, both hands in contact with it — communicates the possessive これは わたしの (this is mine / kosoado near-speaker + possessive). She looks slightly down at the bag with a calm proud expression.
The characters' actions and relationship must make the sentence meaning visually obvious without any text.

Composition: Wide 16:9 shot, 50mm standard lens equivalent (natural perspective).
Characters occupy 60% of the frame.
The young woman is positioned center-frame in slight 3/4 view facing the viewer, full body visible from head to soles of both feet inside the frame. The bag is held at her hip on her left side (viewer's right) — clearly visible as a separate identifiable object but still firmly within her personal possession zone. Both her hands are clearly on the bag.
Eye-level view. Simple minimal background with only essential context.
Main characters are clearly separated from the background in visual contrast.

[STYLE RECIPE — DO NOT CHANGE]
Minimalist flat vector illustration. Clean continuous black outlines with consistent line weight.
Completely flat solid color fills only. Color palette: soft cream off-white background (warm off-white, NOT pure stark white),
deep slate navy outlines, muted warm colors,
warm amber gold accents for emphasis.
This should look like it belongs in a brand style guide, not like AI art. Keep line weights consistent.

[CONSTRAINTS]
No text, no letters, no numbers, no purely decorative symbols inside the image. VISUAL_SYMBOLS entries (question mark / checkmark / X mark / arrow) ARE PERMITTED when explicitly described in [SCENE & ACTION].
No gradients, no shadows, no 3D effects, no photoreal textures.
Apply zero ambient lighting, zero drop shadows, zero global illumination.
